note: Full paid/paywalled article text is intentionally NOT reproduced here. Each entry lists the source site(s), the problem title/URL, and an original one-sentence summary of the core design problem, written independently rather than copied from either site.

problems.name: Blogging Platform (WordPress-style)
problems.summary: Create, publish, and manage blog posts; an entry-level content-management/CRUD design problem.

problems.name: Dropbox / Cloud File Storage
problems.summary: Store, share, and sync files across devices; the interesting problems are uploading/downloading large files efficiently (chunking, resumable transfers), keeping local and remote copies in sync, and controlling access to shared files.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview treats it as an entry-level (Easy) product-design question.

problems.name: Image Hosting Service (Imgur)
problems.summary: Upload, host, and share images; a lighter-weight version of Dropbox/Google Photos-style blob storage and CDN delivery.

problems.name: Local Delivery Service (Gopuff)
problems.summary: Show real-time item availability aggregated across nearby micro-warehouses and let users order without double-booking inventory; centers on fast geo-filtered availability lookups and consistent inventory decrement on checkout.

problems.name: Parking Lot System
problems.summary: Track vehicles, available spots, and payments; a classic object-modeling exercise (often used for both HLD and LLD) more than a scale problem.

problems.name: Pastebin (Text Storage)
problems.summary: Store and share plain-text snippets via a short link; a simpler cousin of the URL-shortener problem focused on text blob storage rather than redirects.

problems.name: Poll/Voting System
problems.summary: Create polls and tally votes in real time; centers on preventing duplicate votes and aggregating results with low latency.

problems.name: QR Code Generator
problems.summary: Generate (optionally trackable) QR codes; a simple encoding/generation service, often paired with URL-shortener-style redirect tracking.

problems.name: To-Do List App
problems.summary: A simple task-tracking CRUD app; a good first system-design warm-up focused on basic API/data-model design rather than scale.

problems.name: URL Shortener
problems.summary: Convert long URLs into short, unique codes and redirect visitors back to the original; the core challenges are collision-free code generation at scale and serving redirects with very low latency given a read-heavy workload.

problems.name: Yelp / Local Business Search
problems.summary: Let users search nearby businesses by location and category and leave reviews; the core problem is efficient geospatial search combined with keeping aggregate ratings fresh and consistent.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview treats it as an entry-level (Easy) product-design question.

problems.name: Airbnb (Hotel Booking)
problems.summary: Search available listings by location/date and book without double-booking; the core problem is availability search combined with concurrency-safe reservation locking.

problems.name: Amazon (E-commerce)
problems.summary: Browse, cart, and purchase products at large catalog/order scale; centers on catalog search, inventory consistency, and order/checkout workflows.

problems.name: Bike/Scooter Sharing System (Lime)
problems.summary: Locate, unlock, and bill dockless vehicles; centers on tracking real-time vehicle location/availability and secure IoT unlock commands.

problems.name: Blogging Platform (Medium-the-site)
problems.summary: Publish and read long-form articles; centers on content storage/rendering and a following/recommendation-driven read feed.

problems.name: CAPTCHA System (reCAPTCHA)
problems.summary: Distinguish humans from bots on forms; involves generating/verifying challenges and increasingly, passive behavioral risk scoring.

problems.name: Coursera (Learning Platform)
problems.summary: Host video courses with progress tracking and quizzes; centers on video delivery plus per-user progress/state tracking at scale.

problems.name: Crash Reporting System (Sentry)
problems.summary: Collect and de-duplicate application crash/error reports; centers on error fingerprinting/grouping and high-volume event ingestion.

problems.name: Cricinfo (Live Sports Scoring)
problems.summary: Real-time ball-by-ball score updates and commentary fan-out to huge concurrent audiences during live matches.

problems.name: Digital Library System
problems.summary: Borrow and read digital books; involves licensing/concurrent-loan limits and content delivery of large e-book/audio files.

problems.name: Digital Signature Service (DocuSign)
problems.summary: Securely route documents for e-signature; involves document workflow state machines and cryptographic signature verification.

problems.name: Distributed Cache (Redis-like)
problems.summary: Build a horizontally-scalable in-memory key-value cache; the core problems are consistent hashing for shard placement, replication for durability, and eviction policy design.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview lists it as Medium.

problems.name: Distributed Counter
problems.summary: A counter that can absorb millions of increments per second (e.g., view counts); centers on sharded counters with periodic aggregation rather than a single hot row.

problems.name: Distributed Job Scheduler
problems.summary: Schedule and reliably execute millions of delayed or recurring jobs (cron-like); the core problems are efficient time-based scheduling at scale and exactly/at-least-once execution guarantees.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview lists it as Medium.

problems.name: Distributed Logging System
problems.summary: An ELK/Splunk-style log pipeline; involves high-throughput log ingestion, indexing for full-text search, and retention/rollup policies.

problems.name: Distributed Rate Limiter
problems.summary: Cap how many requests a client can make in a time window across many stateless API servers; the key problem is sharing counter state (token bucket/sliding window) consistently and cheaply across nodes.

problems.name: DoorDash (Food Delivery)
problems.summary: Match orders to nearby couriers and track delivery in real time; similar in spirit to ride-hailing dispatch but paired with restaurant order/prep workflows.

problems.name: Facebook Live Comments
problems.summary: Stream a very high-throughput, ordered comment feed to many concurrent viewers of a live video; centers on fan-out of a rapid write stream to large numbers of simultaneous readers.

problems.name: Facebook News Feed
problems.summary: Aggregate posts from many sources a user follows into a single ranked, paginated feed; the central tradeoff is precomputing (fan-out on write) versus assembling feeds on demand (fan-out on read).
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview lists it as Medium.

problems.name: Fantasy Sports System
problems.summary: Users draft virtual teams scored against real athlete stats; centers on ingesting live sports data feeds and recomputing scores/rankings in near real time.

problems.name: Feature Flag Management System
problems.summary: Toggle features per user/segment for gradual rollouts and A/B tests; centers on low-latency flag evaluation and fast global config propagation.

problems.name: Game/Contest Leaderboard
problems.summary: Maintain and serve real-time rankings for millions of players; the standard approach uses an in-memory sorted-set structure (e.g. Redis ZSET) for fast rank/score queries at scale.

problems.name: Instagram / Photo Sharing
problems.summary: Users upload photos, follow others, and view a feed; key challenges are efficient feed generation (fan-out on write vs read) and serving media at scale via blob storage and a CDN.

problems.name: Local Food/Restaurant Review App
problems.summary: A Yelp-style app scoped to restaurants; the practice problem centers on the same geospatial search plus aggregate-rating problems as general local business review systems.

problems.name: Location History Service
problems.summary: Store and query a user's historical location trail (Google Timeline-style); involves efficient time-indexed geospatial storage and range queries.

problems.name: Malware Scanning Service
problems.summary: Scan uploaded files for malware; involves an async scanning pipeline that can hold/quarantine files until a verdict is reached without blocking uploads.

problems.name: NFT Marketplace (OpenSea)
problems.summary: Buy/sell/mint unique digital tokens; combines blockchain transaction handling with a conventional marketplace search/listing layer.

problems.name: Nearby Friends
problems.summary: Share real-time location with a friend list; centers on efficient proximity queries plus privacy-aware, opt-in live location updates.

problems.name: News Aggregator (Google News)
problems.summary: Ingest articles from many publishers, cluster near-duplicate stories, and rank them into topics; involves large-scale ingestion, near-duplicate/clustering detection, and personalized ranking.

problems.name: Newsletter Platform (Substack)
problems.summary: Compose and send newsletters to subscriber lists; centers on high-volume transactional email delivery and subscriber list management.

problems.name: Online Auction
problems.summary: Run time-boxed bidding on items where the highest bid wins; the core challenge is processing concurrent bids with strict ordering and correctness as the auction clock runs out.

problems.name: Online Judge (LeetCode)
problems.summary: Accept submitted code, execute it safely against test cases, and return verdicts; the hard parts are sandboxing untrusted code execution and scaling a queue of compute-heavy grading jobs.

problems.name: Password Manager (LastPass)
problems.summary: Securely store and sync user credentials; centers on client-side/zero-knowledge encryption so the server never sees plaintext secrets.

problems.name: Plagiarism Checker (Turnitin)
problems.summary: Detect copied text across a huge corpus of documents; centers on near-duplicate detection using shingling/fingerprinting techniques (similar to search indexing).

problems.name: Podcast Hosting Platform
problems.summary: Host and distribute podcast audio via RSS-like feeds; shares media-storage/CDN concerns with simpler catalog/subscription needs than full video streaming.

problems.name: Price Tracking / Comparison Service
problems.summary: Continuously scrape or ingest prices from external sites/sources, store price history, and alert users on drops; hinges on scalable scheduled scraping/ingestion and efficient time-series storage.

problems.name: Reddit (Discussion Forum)
problems.summary: Threaded discussions with voting-based ranking across many communities; centers on scalable comment-tree storage and vote-based ranking algorithms.

problems.name: Smart Home Automation System
problems.summary: Control and monitor connected home devices; involves reliable low-latency device command/telemetry messaging (similar to IoT fleet management at a smaller scale).

problems.name: Splitwise (Expense Sharing)
problems.summary: Track shared expenses between people and simplify who-owes-whom; the core problem is a debt-simplification algorithm over a graph of pairwise balances.

problems.name: Spotify (Audio Streaming)
problems.summary: Stream licensed audio and build playlists/recommendations; involves CDN-backed streaming delivery and a recommendation pipeline over listening history.

problems.name: StackOverflow (Q&A Platform)
problems.summary: Ask/answer questions with voting-based ranking; shares Reddit-style vote aggregation with added reputation and duplicate-question detection concerns.

problems.name: Strava / Fitness Tracking
problems.summary: Record and share GPS-tracked workout routes and let others react/comment; involves efficient storage of large GPS trace data and computing route-based leaderboards (segments).

problems.name: Survey System (SurveyMonkey)
problems.summary: Build surveys and collect/aggregate responses; involves flexible form schema storage and real-time response aggregation.

problems.name: Telemedicine Platform (Practo)
problems.summary: Book and conduct online doctor consultations; combines appointment scheduling with real-time video-call infrastructure.

problems.name: Ticketmaster / Event Ticket Booking
problems.summary: Sell a limited number of seats for popular events without double-booking or overselling under extreme concurrent demand; centers on reservation locking, queueing, and inventory consistency.

problems.name: Tinder / Location-based Matching
problems.summary: Show nearby candidate profiles and record mutual likes as matches; revolves around efficient proximity queries (geohashing/quadtrees) and avoiding showing the same profile twice.

problems.name: Translation Service (Google Translate)
problems.summary: Machine translation at scale; involves routing text to translation models and caching common translations for latency/cost savings.

problems.name: Truecaller (Caller Identification)
problems.summary: Identify unknown callers and block spam using crowdsourced number data; centers on a huge lookup index and spam-scoring aggregation.

problems.name: Two-Factor Authentication System (Authy)
problems.summary: Provide a second auth factor via SMS/email/TOTP; centers on time-based one-time code generation and reliable multi-channel delivery.

problems.name: Typeahead Suggestion
problems.summary: Real-time search-as-you-type suggestions; classically solved with a trie or precomputed top-N suggestions per prefix, served from an in-memory index.

problems.name: Unique ID Generator (Snowflake)
problems.summary: Generate globally unique, roughly time-sortable IDs without central coordination; the classic solution encodes timestamp + worker ID + sequence into one ID.

problems.name: UrbanCompany (Home Services)
problems.summary: Marketplace matching customers with local service professionals; centers on availability/scheduling matching similar to ride-hailing dispatch.

problems.name: Video Transcoding Service
problems.summary: Convert uploaded video into multiple formats/resolutions; centers on a scalable async transcoding job pipeline (similar to the YouTube upload path).

problems.name: Virtual Private Network (VPN)
problems.summary: Provide secure, private network tunnels for clients; centers on encrypted tunneling protocols and secure key exchange.

problems.name: Virtual Waiting Room
problems.summary: Queue excess users during traffic surges before admitting them to the main system; centers on fair queueing and controlled admission rate to protect backend capacity.

problems.name: Weather Forecasting System
problems.summary: Ingest sensor/satellite data and serve forecasts; centers on large-scale geospatial data ingestion and serving derived forecast queries efficiently.

problems.name: WhatsApp / Real-time Chat
problems.summary: Deliver one-on-one and group messages in real time with delivery/read receipts; centers on maintaining persistent connections at scale (WebSockets), message ordering, and offline delivery guarantees.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview lists it as Medium.

problems.name: Wikipedia (Knowledge Base)
problems.summary: A collaboratively edited encyclopedia; involves revision history storage, edit-conflict handling, and read-heavy full-text search.

problems.name: YouTube / Video Streaming
problems.summary: Support uploading, transcoding, and streaming video to a huge audience; centers on async transcoding pipelines, adaptive bitrate streaming, and CDN-backed delivery.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Hard; HelloInterview lists it as Medium.

problems.name: Ad Click Aggregator
problems.summary: Aggregate huge volumes of ad click/impression events in near real time for billing and analytics; centers on stream processing, windowed aggregation, and exactly-once counting under high write throughput.

problems.name: B-Tree Storage Engine
problems.summary: The classic storage engine behind Postgres/MySQL; centers on page-based balanced-tree layout optimized for read-heavy, in-place-update workloads.

problems.name: Blockchain Ledger (Bitcoin)
problems.summary: A decentralized, tamper-resistant ledger; centers on consensus among untrusted nodes (proof-of-work) and cryptographic chaining of blocks.

problems.name: Browser Engine (Chrome)
problems.summary: The core rendering engine of a web browser; centers on parsing HTML/CSS into a render tree and efficiently laying out/painting pixels.

problems.name: Build System (Bazel)
problems.summary: A hermetic, reproducible build system for large monorepos; centers on dependency-graph-based incremental builds and build caching.

problems.name: CDN (Content Delivery Network)
problems.summary: Serve static assets with low latency worldwide; involves edge cache placement, origin fetch/invalidation, and request routing to the nearest healthy edge node.

problems.name: CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions)
problems.summary: Run arbitrary build/test/deploy workflows triggered by code changes; centers on a scalable, isolated (containerized) job execution fleet and a DAG-based workflow scheduler.

problems.name: Canva (Online Graphic Design)
problems.summary: Collaborative design canvas with many asset types; shares real-time collaboration concerns with Google Docs, plus asset/version management.

problems.name: Change Data Capture (CDC) System
problems.summary: Propagate database changes to downstream systems in real time; centers on reading a database's write-ahead log and reliably streaming change events.

problems.name: ChatGPT-style LLM Serving
problems.summary: Serve a conversational AI product to a huge user base; involves streaming token-by-token responses, GPU inference fleet scaling/batching, and managing long conversation context.

problems.name: CodeSandbox (Online IDE)
problems.summary: In-browser code editing and execution; centers on sandboxed/isolated code execution environments and real-time preview updates.

problems.name: Collaborative Whiteboard (Miro)
problems.summary: Real-time shared canvas of shapes/drawings; another CRDT/OT-style real-time sync problem, similar in spirit to Google Docs but for spatial objects.

problems.name: Crowdfunding / Donations Platform
problems.summary: Let creators collect pledges toward a funding goal with a deadline; the tricky part is handling payment capture/refunds correctly if a goal isn't met, under concurrent pledges.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Medium; HelloInterview's related practice problem is rated Hard.

problems.name: DDoS Protection System
problems.summary: Detect and absorb distributed denial-of-service traffic; centers on traffic anomaly detection and massive scrubbing capacity at the network edge.

problems.name: DFS Metadata Service (NameNode)
problems.summary: The metadata component of a distributed file system (HDFS-style); centers on tracking file-to-block-to-node mappings and coordinating replication/recovery.

problems.name: Data Lakehouse (Delta Lake)
problems.summary: Bring ACID transactions to big-data storage on object stores; centers on a transaction log layered over immutable files in blob storage.

problems.name: Digital Wallet
problems.summary: Peer-to-peer money transfer app; shares payment-system concerns (idempotency, ledger consistency) with added focus on wallet balance management.

problems.name: Discord (Voice & Chat)
problems.summary: Persistent community text/voice servers at scale; combines real-time chat fan-out with low-latency voice routing similar to a chat + video-conferencing hybrid.

problems.name: Distributed Configuration System (Etcd)
problems.summary: Strongly consistent config/service-discovery store; centers on consensus (Raft) to keep a small amount of critical config consistent cluster-wide.

problems.name: Distributed File System
problems.summary: An HDFS/GFS-style system for storing huge datasets across machines; centers on chunked storage, replication, and a metadata service tracking chunk locations.

problems.name: Distributed Key-Value Store
problems.summary: A DynamoDB/Cassandra-style store; centers on consistent hashing for partitioning, replication, and tunable consistency (quorum reads/writes).

problems.name: Distributed Lock Manager
problems.summary: A Chubby/Zookeeper-style coordination service; centers on consensus (Paxos/Raft) for leader election and fault-tolerant distributed locks.

problems.name: Distributed SQL Database (CockroachDB)
problems.summary: A horizontally-scalable SQL database with strong consistency; centers on distributed transactions (e.g. Raft-replicated ranges) while preserving ACID semantics.

problems.name: Distributed Tracing System (Jaeger)
problems.summary: Trace a request as it flows across microservices; involves span collection, sampling strategies, and reconstructing full traces from distributed spans.

problems.name: Distributed Transaction Manager
problems.summary: Coordinate transactions across multiple microservices; centers on two-phase commit versus saga-based compensating-transaction patterns.

problems.name: Domain Name System (DNS)
problems.summary: Resolve hostnames to IP addresses hierarchically; involves a globally distributed, heavily cached, eventually-consistent lookup hierarchy.

problems.name: Facebook Post Search
problems.summary: Full-text search over billions of social posts with strict privacy/visibility rules per viewer; the hard part is combining a scalable inverted-index search layer with per-query access-control filtering.

problems.name: Flash Sale System
problems.summary: Handle extreme concurrency against very limited inventory for short sale windows; centers on avoiding overselling under massive simultaneous demand (queueing, inventory reservation).

problems.name: Garbage Collector
problems.summary: Automatic memory management for a language runtime; centers on tracing-based reachability algorithms (mark-sweep, generational, concurrent collectors).

problems.name: Generic Recommendation System
problems.summary: Serve personalized content recommendations; involves offline model training combined with a low-latency online serving/ranking layer.

problems.name: GitHub (Version Control Hosting)
problems.summary: Host Git repositories at scale; centers on efficient storage of the Git object model and scaling repository read/write operations.

problems.name: Gmail (Email Service)
problems.summary: Send/receive/store email at scale; involves reliable message delivery (SMTP-like queuing), large mailbox storage, and full-text search over messages.

problems.name: Google Calendar (Scheduling)
problems.summary: Manage events, recurring schedules, and free/busy lookups across users; centers on efficient recurring-event expansion and conflict-free scheduling queries.

problems.name: Google Docs / Collaborative Editing
problems.summary: Allow many users to edit the same document simultaneously and converge on the same result; the classic solutions are Operational Transformation or CRDTs plus real-time sync over WebSockets.

problems.name: Google Maps (Location Service)
problems.summary: Provide maps, geocoding, and turn-by-turn routing; the hard part is efficient graph-based shortest-path routing over a planet-scale road network plus live traffic data.

problems.name: Google Photos (Cloud Photo Storage)
problems.summary: Store and organize large photo/video libraries with search; shares blob-storage concerns with Dropbox plus ML-driven content search (faces, objects).

problems.name: Graph Database (Neo4j)
problems.summary: Store and traverse richly connected data; centers on storage layouts optimized for graph traversal rather than tabular joins.

problems.name: IRCTC (Railway Booking)
problems.summary: Handle extreme traffic spikes (Tatkal-style rushes) for a fixed seat inventory; shares flash-sale-style overselling concerns under massive simultaneous demand.

problems.name: Identity Provider (Auth0)
problems.summary: Centralized identity and access management (SSO); involves implementing standard auth protocols (OAuth/OIDC/SAML) and token issuance/validation at scale.

problems.name: IoT Fleet Management System
problems.summary: Monitor and manage many remote IoT devices; involves high-volume device telemetry ingestion and reliable command/control messaging to devices.

problems.name: JavaScript Runtime (Node.js)
problems.summary: An async, event-driven JS runtime; centers on the single-threaded event loop model and non-blocking I/O scheduling.

problems.name: LSM Tree Storage Engine
problems.summary: The write-optimized storage engine behind RocksDB/Cassandra; centers on memtable + sorted-run compaction design tradeoffs between write and read amplification.

problems.name: LinkedIn (Professional Network)
problems.summary: A professional social graph with feeds and connection requests; combines social-graph storage with feed ranking similar to News Feed-style problems.

problems.name: Live Streaming Platform (Twitch)
problems.summary: Real-time video ingestion from broadcasters fanned out to many viewers; shares transcoding/CDN concerns with YouTube plus much lower end-to-end latency requirements.

problems.name: Load Balancer (NGINX)
problems.summary: A high-performance reverse proxy/load balancer; centers on connection handling models and load-balancing algorithms (round robin, least-connections, consistent hashing).

problems.name: MapReduce Framework
problems.summary: A batch distributed-processing framework; centers on splitting work into map/reduce stages, shuffling intermediate data, and fault-tolerant re-execution of failed tasks.

problems.name: Message Queue (Kafka)
problems.summary: A distributed log-based pub/sub system; centers on partitioned, ordered, replicated logs with consumer offset tracking.

problems.name: Metrics Monitoring & Alerting
problems.summary: Collect, store, and query huge volumes of time-series metrics and trigger alerts; relies on efficient time-series storage/compression and a scalable ingestion + query path.

problems.name: Object Storage Service (Amazon S3)
problems.summary: Durable, scalable blob storage; centers on data replication/erasure coding for durability and a metadata layer for object lookup.

problems.name: Onion Routing Network (Tor)
problems.summary: Anonymous communication via layered encryption across relays; centers on multi-hop circuit construction where no single relay knows both source and destination.

problems.name: Online Chess
problems.summary: Match players in real time and referee a live game with move validation; centers on low-latency bidirectional game state sync and reliable reconnect/resume handling.

problems.name: Payment Processing System
problems.summary: Move money between parties reliably; the central concerns are idempotency for retried requests, strong consistency/auditability of ledger state, and integrating with external banking rails.

problems.name: Peer-to-Peer File Sharing (BitTorrent)
problems.summary: Decentralized file distribution among peers; centers on chunked file exchange, peer discovery (trackers/DHT), and tit-for-tat incentive mechanisms.

problems.name: Pinterest (Visual Discovery)
problems.summary: An image-discovery feed with boards/pins; combines image storage/CDN delivery with a recommendation-driven discovery feed.

problems.name: Platform as a Service (Heroku)
problems.summary: Build/run/deploy arbitrary apps in the cloud; centers on container orchestration, build pipelines, and multi-tenant resource isolation.

problems.name: Privacy-Preserving Analytics System
problems.summary: Collect usage analytics without exposing individual user data; centers on differential-privacy techniques (noise injection, aggregation) applied to raw event data.

problems.name: Push Notification System
problems.summary: Deliver notifications to millions of users across channels (push, email, SMS); the key problems are per-channel fan-out at scale, user preference/throttling rules, and delivery retries.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Medium; HelloInterview's related practice problem is rated Hard.

problems.name: Real-time Ad Bidding System
problems.summary: A programmatic ad auction that must respond within tens of milliseconds; centers on extremely tight-latency bid evaluation across many concurrent auctions.

problems.name: Real-time Collaborative Spreadsheet
problems.summary: A Google Sheets-style shared spreadsheet; adds formula dependency-graph recalculation on top of the usual real-time collaborative-editing (CRDT/OT) problem.

problems.name: Real-time Fraud Detection System
problems.summary: Score and block fraudulent transactions as they happen; centers on low-latency feature computation and rule/ML scoring within the transaction path.

problems.name: Robinhood / Stock Trading
problems.summary: Let users view live market data and place trades; centers on fanning out fast-changing price ticks to many subscribers and handling order placement with strong consistency guarantees.

problems.name: Secrets Management System (Vault)
problems.summary: Securely store and grant access to secrets (API keys, certs); centers on encryption-at-rest, fine-grained access policies, and short-lived dynamic credentials.

problems.name: Serverless Compute Platform (AWS Lambda)
problems.summary: Run code on-demand in response to events; the hard part is fast cold-start container provisioning and per-invocation isolation at scale.

problems.name: Service Mesh (Istio)
problems.summary: Infrastructure layer for service-to-service traffic; centers on sidecar proxy architecture for routing, retries, and observability without app code changes.

problems.name: Stock Exchange
problems.summary: A matching engine for buy/sell orders; the hard part is a deterministic, low-latency order-matching algorithm plus durable, auditable trade logs.

problems.name: Supply Chain Management System
problems.summary: Track goods from manufacturing to delivery; centers on modeling multi-stage state transitions and providing end-to-end shipment traceability.

problems.name: Time-Series Database (InfluxDB)
problems.summary: Optimized storage/queries for timestamped metrics; centers on write-optimized storage layout, compression, and time-windowed aggregation queries.

problems.name: Top-K / Trending Elements

sources.title: Design a Top-K Frequent Elements Service

problems.summary: Continuously surface the most frequent items (views, hashtags) from a high-volume real-time stream; relies on approximate counting structures (count-min sketch, heavy hitters) and windowed aggregation.
problems.note: ScaleMock lists this as Medium; HelloInterview lists it as Hard.

problems.name: Traffic Control System
problems.summary: Optimize smart-city traffic light flow; involves real-time sensor ingestion and a control loop that adjusts signals based on live conditions.

problems.name: Uber / Ride Hailing
problems.summary: Match nearby riders and drivers in real time and track trips; the hard problems are efficient proximity search over moving drivers, matching under contention, and consistent trip-state updates.

problems.name: VPN Protocol (WireGuard)
problems.summary: A modern, minimal VPN protocol; centers on efficient cryptographic handshakes and low-overhead encrypted tunneling.

problems.name: Vector Database (Pinecone)
problems.summary: Store and query high-dimensional embedding vectors; centers on approximate nearest-neighbor indexing (e.g. HNSW) for fast similarity search.

problems.name: Virtual Machine (JVM)
problems.summary: A process VM executing bytecode; centers on bytecode interpretation/JIT compilation and automatic memory management.

problems.name: Web Application Firewall (WAF)
problems.summary: Inspect and filter malicious HTTP traffic in the request path; centers on low-latency rule/pattern matching against a stream of live requests.

problems.name: Web Crawler
problems.summary: Crawl and index a large portion of the web; the core problems are URL frontier management, politeness/dedup at scale, and distributing crawl work across many workers.

problems.name: Web Search Engine (Google Search)
problems.summary: Index the web and rank results for a query; combines a distributed crawler, an inverted-index build pipeline, and a relevance-ranking layer.

problems.name: Workflow Orchestration Engine (Airflow)
problems.summary: Author, schedule, and monitor multi-step data workflows; centers on DAG-based scheduling with retries, backfills, and dependency management.

problems.name: Zepto (Quick Commerce)
problems.summary: 10-minute grocery delivery; a tighter-SLA variant of local delivery/dispatch problems, emphasizing dark-store inventory and very fast courier assignment.

problems.name: Zoom (Video Conferencing)
problems.summary: Real-time multi-party audio/video calls; centers on low-latency media routing (SFU/MCU architectures), NAT traversal, and adaptive bitrate under variable network conditions.
